title: Anil tetragonoloba
taxonomy: Anil tetragonoloba is classified as a type of legume, belonging to the family Fabaceae. This family encompasses a wide range of plants, commonly known as beans or peas. The genus Anil is a part of the tribe Millettieae, which includes various species of vetches and other legumes. The classification of Anil tetragonoloba is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its compound leaves and pod structure. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification within the Fabaceae family.

intro: Anil tetragonoloba, also known as Indian bloodroot or Anil, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The health benefits of Anil tetragonoloba include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in treating various ailments such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Anil tetragonoloba is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
